Container.Name: nfsen-ng
Container.Overview: nfsen-ng is a modern, in-place replacement for the ageing NfSen web frontend. It sits on top of the nfdump tools and visualises NetFlow / sFlow / IPFIX traffic with real-time SSE push, a responsive light/dark UI, Sankey traffic-flow diagrams, and RRD or VictoriaMetrics storage. This is the WEB UI. It only displays flow data that a collector has already written to its data directory. Install the companion "nfsen-ng-nfcapd" collector template (or run the bundled Compose stack) and mount BOTH containers on the SAME appdata path so the UI can read what the collector writes. A device on your network (pfSense/OPNsense/MikroTik/…) must export flows to the collector. The app does not terminate TLS by design; front it with SWAG, Nginx Proxy Manager, or Traefik.
Container.Requires: A NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX collector must write into the same data directory this container reads. Use the companion nfsen-ng-nfcapd template and point both at the same appdata path (default /mnt/user/appdata/nfsen-ng). Your router/switch must export flows to the collector's UDP port.

Config.Name: Web UI
Config.Description: nfsen-ng web interface (OpenSwoole HTTP). Front with a reverse proxy for TLS.

Config.Name: Data (profiles)
Config.Description: nfdump profiles/flow-file directory. MUST be the same path the collector writes to.

Config.Name: App data
Config.Description: nfsen-ng's own data: RRD database (rrd/) plus saved preferences and alert rules/state/log (state/). Keep this on a persistent path so it survives image updates.

Config.Name: Sources
Config.Description: Comma-separated source names. Each must exist as live/<name> under the data directory — i.e. match the collector's output sub-directory.

Config.Name: Tracked ports
Config.Description: Comma-separated port numbers tracked per source in RRD.

Config.Name: nfdump profiles path
Config.Description: Container-side profiles path. Must equal the 'Data (profiles)' mount target above.

Config.Name: nfdump binary
Config.Description: Path to the nfdump binary inside the container.

Config.Name: Log level
Config.Description: DEBUG, INFO, NOTICE, WARNING, ERR, CRIT, ALERT or EMERG.

Config.Name: Import years
Config.Description: Backfill window (years) processed on first start.
